name: Ascent to Toloño from Labastida – Toloño Mountains
name_original: Ascensión al Toloño desde Labastida — Sierra de Toloño
description:
El Toloño is one of the most emblematic peaks in this area of Spain and partly separates La Rioja and Euskadi. As a consequence, it also acts as a natural border between the Mediterranean and Atlantic climates.

There are several ways to ascend to the top of Toloño. We have chosen the itinerary that starts from Labastida and goes up its south face to the top and then turns around and goes down its east face. In general, you must be in good physical shape, since the ascent is quite hard (there are 600 meters of elevation gain in just 4 kilometers). Luckily, the descent is much gentler, but it is also longer.

The Route can be done at any time of the year, although in autumn the beech trees that grow in some parts of the mountains are dressed in incredible colors and make the landscape even more beautiful. To get to Labastida, there are several buses from Logroño and Vitoria-Gasteiz, but it is best to use your own vehicle.
region_intro: Hiking around Labastida/Bastida offers diverse terrain, situated between the Sierra de Cantabria mountain range and the Ebro River. The region features prominent peaks like Mount Toloño, which provides significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Extensive vineyards characterize the lower elevations, contributing to the area's landscape. This combination of mountains, river valleys, and agricultural land creates varied hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Labastida/Bastida The most popular hiking route is Ascent to Toloño from Labastida – Toloño Mountains, a difficult 11.6 miles (18.6 km) trail that takes 6 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route involves a challenging ascent to Mount Toloño, offering expansive vistas of the Rioja Alavesa region. Another top favourite among local hikers is Toloño (geodetic vertex) – Toloño loop from Labastida / Bastida, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This loop leads to the geodetic vertex of Toloño, providing views across the landscape. Local hikers also love the Humilladero Hermitage loop from Labastida / Bastida, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Labastida/Bastida is defined by the Sierra de Cantabria mountains, the Ebro River valley, and extensive vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from demanding mountain ascents to gentler vineyard walks. The routes in Labastida/Bastida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Labastida/Bastida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Labastida/Bastida?
A: Labastida/Bastida offers a diverse selection of over 35 hiking routes. These range from easy strolls through vineyards to challenging ascents in the Sierra de Cantabria mountains.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Labastida/Bastida?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, from the dramatic mountain views of the Sierra de Cantabria to the picturesque vineyard paths, and the blend of natural beauty with historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Labastida/Bastida?
A: The region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes, especially during the grape harvest season in the vineyards. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter offers crisp air and unique views, though some higher mountain paths might require more preparation.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Labastida/Bastida?
A: Yes, there are 6 easy routes available, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These often traverse the lower elevations and vineyards, offering gentle gradients and charming scenery.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Labastida/Bastida?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, there are 8 difficult routes, many of which involve significant ascents into the Sierra de Cantabria. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497797950/ascent-to-tolono-from-labastida-tolono-mountains" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Toloño from Labastida – Toloño Mountains&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 11.6-mile trail with substantial elevation gain, offering panoramic views from Mount Toloño.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Labastida/Bastida?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20503256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toloño (geodetic vertex) – Toloño loop from Labastida / Bastida&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.7-mile loop that takes you to the geodetic vertex of Toloño, offering expansive views.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls near Labastida/Bastida?
A: Yes, the region boasts several beautiful waterfalls. You can explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5474808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ollerías Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6864843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Inglares Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are often part of scenic routes, particularly along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2905300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Labastida/Bastida?
A: Yes, Labastida/Bastida offers routes suitable for families. Areas like San Ginés Park provide natural spaces with recreational facilities, and many of the easier vineyard walks are perfect for families with children. Look for routes with lower difficulty grades and shorter distances.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hikers can enjoy stunning natural landmarks and viewpoints. Mount Toloño offers panoramic vistas of the Rioja Alavesa, the Ebro River, and distant mountain ranges. The Sierra de Cantabria provides dramatic backdrops, and the extensive vineyards create picturesque landscapes throughout the region.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural attractions accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the trails often lead to or pass by significant historical and cultural sites. The ruins of the ancient Monastery of Santa María del Toloño are found at the base of Mount Toloño. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38282992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humilladero Hermitage loop from Labastida / Bastida&lt;/a&gt; takes you past a historical hermitage. You can also find old wine presses carved into rocks, showcasing the area's winemaking heritage, and the Santo Cristo Chapel offers bird's-eye views of Labastida.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Labastida/Bastida?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Labastida/Bastida, particularly near popular trailheads or within the town itself. Many routes, such as those leading to Mount Toloño, often have designated parking areas at their starting points, like near San Ginés Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Labastida/Bastida?
A: The terrain varies significantly, from gentle, well-maintained paths through vineyards and along the Ebro River to rugged, rocky trails with steep ascents and descents in the Sierra de Cantabria. Some mountain routes require good fitness and sure-footedness, while others are more accessible for casual walkers.
tips: A short climb to the hill where the hermitage is located has the reward of the view, as well as several possibilities for excursions on foot starting from the Plaza del Olmo, which is nearby.
The ruins don't say much, but they are a great place to breathe a little after the very hard climb from San Ginés.

Very curious environment, a small reward after the slope of the low forest that you have to cross to reach it. From here, on a small slope, we climb to the Toloño cross and, if you wish, to the nearby geodesic vertex, both with great views.
The summit of Toloño is more spectacular but, once there, it is a good idea to go to the geodesic vertex, which is very close.
